the equilibrium size for part one requires solving zero equals zero point one times P times one minus P over one thousand minus ten minus five sine of pi t over six which yields no single constant value because h varies with time so we look for average or specific moments but for part two the carrying capacity drops linearly as one thousand minus three t and the population becomes unsustainable when the carrying capacity falls below the minimum harvesting rate which is ten turtles per year so we set one thousand minus three t equal to ten and solve for t giving three t equals nine hundred ninety so t equals three hundred thirty years when the population can no longer sustain itself and the equilibrium becomes non positive
